title: AIMS qualitative analysis artifact (draft)
description: Schema reference for the planned validate_qualitative.py. Validation is implemented in Python rather than a JSON Schema library to avoid runtime dependencies. Draft contract defined by issue #89; implemented by #92 and gated by #93.

optional_input_hashes_keys.calendar_sha256: SHA-256 of the calendar artifact (#91). Null or absent until calendars exist.

optional_metadata_keys.gates: Gate-pass summary written by the #93 gate pass: counts of gated entries and whether the market narrative was withheld. Absent before the gate pass runs.

optional_market_keys.numeric_claims: List of {"value": number, "unit": "percent"|"price"|"count"|"other", "refers_to": <feature name or evidence ID>}. Declares every numeric token appearing in market.narrative free text; verified by the #93 numeric-claims gate the same way as driver-level numeric_claims.

optional_theme_keys.numeric_claims: Same shape and verification as market numeric_claims, declaring numeric tokens appearing in themes[].title.

optional_instrument_keys.qualitative_gates: List of gate names attached by the #93 gate pass, mirroring the risk_gates pattern. Entries with a non-empty list are excluded from rendering.

optional_driver_keys.direction_claim: Structured factual claim about realized price action: {"direction": "up"|"down"|"none", "window": "1d"|"5d"|"20d"|"60d"}. "up"/"down" are checked by the #93 direction-consistency gate against the sign of the matching return feature; "none" explicitly asserts no notable price movement and is checked against that feature falling within a near-zero tolerance band (threshold defined by #93). Omit the field entirely when the driver does not address price direction at all.
optional_driver_keys.numeric_claims: List of {"value": number, "unit": "percent"|"price"|"count"|"other", "refers_to": <feature name or evidence ID>}. Every numeric token appearing in driver text must be declared here; the #93 numeric-claims gate verifies each entry against the quantitative artifact or the cited evidence item within tolerance.

notes.generated_at: Analysis-date midnight UTC, mirroring the analysis artifact; never wall-clock time.
notes.analysis_date: YYYY-MM-DD; must match the analysis and evidence artifacts the hashes point at.
notes.interval: Bar interval ('d'/'w'/'m') carried over from the analysis artifact's metadata.config.interval. Flat like the evidence bundle's metadata.interval (not nested), so the qualitative artifact's own filename stem ('d' -> no suffix, else '-{interval}') stays correct for manual weekly/monthly dispatches without depending on the analysis/history-specific config.interval shape.
notes.model_id: Claude model ID used by Claude Code Action. Pinned in code; recorded here for provenance.
notes.execution_type: Always 'claude-code-action-oauth' for subscription OAuth execution; no credential is recorded.
notes.action_revision: Full commit SHA of anthropics/claude-code-action used by the workflow.
notes.prompt_version: Version string of the committed prompt file; prompt_sha256 is the SHA-256 of that file's bytes.
notes.stance: Relation of the qualitative evidence to the quantitative signal for this instrument. 'conflicting' (disagreeing with the signal) is legitimate output and is never gated for the disagreement itself; only factual misstatements of realized price action are gated.
notes.instruments: Entries cover only the top-K published signals (K=5, matching history.py DEFAULT_TOP_K), keyed by canonical_id present in the day's analysis artifact.
notes.citations: Lists of evidence IDs that must exist in the evidence bundle named by input_hashes.evidence_sha256. Every driver, theme, and the market narrative must carry at least one citation.
notes.free_text: Free text (driver text, narrative, theme titles) is display-only. It may not contain numeric tokens undeclared in numeric_claims, with a whitelist for feature names (e.g. ret_20d, ma50_dist, rsi_14), ISO dates, and — when the analysis artifact is supplied for cross-checking — numerals embedded in covered instruments' display_name (e.g. the '500' in 'S&P 500', the '100' in 'NASDAQ 100' or 'FTSE 100', '225' in 'Nikkei 225', '50' in 'Euro Stoxx 50', '40' in 'CAC 40', '2000' in 'Russell 2000'), so reciting a covered instrument's proper name is never mistaken for an undeclared numeric claim.
notes.length_caps: narrative <= 1200 chars; driver text <= 300 chars; theme title <= 120 chars; <= 5 themes; <= 4 drivers per instrument; <= 6 citations per driver.
